DISCOVERY 610, TENTATIVE ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART: A. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15, AND 16, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE A VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE THAT RESPONDS MORE SPECIFICALLY TO THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IN EACH INTERROGATORY; B. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS.3, 8, 11, 14, 17, 19, AND 21, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE A VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE THAT IDENTIFIES DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE SPECIFIC ISSUE, CONTENTION OR REQUEST IN EACH INTERROGATORY; C. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT; COURT REPORTER: PATRICK HEARD, CSR # 11055

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogoatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Lisa R. Gorman, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Fairhaven Group, Inc.
Charles J. Wisch, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Sandy Rouse

After hearing, Tentative adopted. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories, set one, and request for sanctions, granted in part, denied in part:
a. As to special interrogatory nos. 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15, and 16, plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response that responds more specifically to the information sought in each interrogatory;
b. As to special interrogatory nos.3, 8, 11, 14, 17, 19, and 21, plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response that identifies documents responsive to the specific issue, contention or request in each interrogatory;
c. Request for sanctions denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit; Court Reporter: Patrick Heard, CSR # 11055

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoena And For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, Plaintiff Sandy Rouse's motion (1) to quash subpoena, and (2) for protective order: off calendar, moot. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Lisa R. Gorman, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Fairhaven Group, Inc.
Charles J. Wisch, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Sandy Rouse

After hearing, Defendant Fairhaven Group, Inc.'s motion to compel further response and documents to request for production of documents, no. 26, set one; and for monetary sanctions is taken under submission. Commissioner: Paul Slavit; Court Reporter: Patrick Heard, CSR # 11055
